Department Labor ("DOL") actively investigating these plans, focusing both promoters such plans employers sponsor them.
believes that opting workers compensation insurance disservice workers because employers then provide lower fewer benefits, make more difficult qualify benefits, control access doctors limit independent claims appeals.
This view strict contrast rationale employers sponsor such plans that opting requires employees take more responsibility workplace safety, provides treatment more quickly, cuts costs avoids protracted expensive litigation.
experience, most large employers believe that they better than Texas workers' compensation system ensuring that employees injured receive appropriate benefits, both medical wage loss, through ERISA occupational injury benefit plan.
investigation ERISA occupational injury benefit plan, will likely perform on-site investigation serve employer with subpoena.
Generally, focus investigation ensure plan's compliance with ERISA (e.g., there written plan document summary plan description, Form 5500s being properly filed, etc.)
determine whether benefit claims being improperly denied (e.g., ERISA claims rules being followed, requirements benefits being uniformly applied, etc.).
typically asks multitude documents ranging from plan documents detailed ERISA claim files.
Action Item: Employers sponsor occupational injury benefit plans should review those plans make sure they properly documented being operated accordance with ERISA.
addition, such employers should ensure that denial benefits properly documented complies with ERISA's claims rules.
Because most employers outsource claim processing third party administrator, this compliance check will necessarily involve working with outside providers ensure that they complying with ERISA making benefit determinations.
Finally, employers receive notice investigation served with subpoena should consult with counsel preparing their response.

now, subject Julie Enszer's latest poetry book.
Lilith's Demons, Enszer's third book, reimagines infamous, ancient figure Lilith modern, empowered woman.
Split into three parts, book begins with poems from Lilith's point view, followed middle section narrated demons (all with their unique name personality), finally, closing with short section narrated angels drawn Lilith's alluring power.
Through eyes Lilith demons, Enszer crafts beautiful thought-provoking narrative modern issue women's oppression, punishment they receive.
